food_name: Churros and Drink Combo for Two
aliases: Churros and Drink Set for Two

english: Churros and Drink Set for Two

food_subgroup: Fried dough pastry with beverage combo

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has an entry for churro (FOODON_00002694) as the primary food component. The combo meal format (churros + beverage) has no exact FoodOn code; closest_parent applied to the dominant solid food component.
ontology.foodex2_description: Doughnuts and similar fried dough products
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers fried dough products as the closest parent for churros. No specific churros code exists in FoodEx2; the beverage component is not separately coded here as the combo is treated as a single record anchored to the dominant solid component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT food concept exists for churros or this combo format.

main_ingredients: beverage (type unspecified — may be hot chocolate, coffee, or soft drink)

serving.description: 1 combo for 2 persons (2 churros portions + 2 drinks)
serving.natural_language_portion: One combo for two persons consisting of approximately two standard churros portions (estimated 120 g each, totalling 240 g of churros) plus two beverages (estimated 80 g liquid equivalent each for nutrient averaging purposes), giving a combined estimated weight of approximately 400 g for the full combo.

nutrient_source.title: Churros, plain, fried — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18617) and USDA Foundation Foods; beverage component estimated as hot chocolate using USDA FoodData Central entry for hot cocoa prepared with water
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are a weighted composite estimate. Churros component (60% of total weight, 240 g of 400 g) is based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for churros (NDB 18617): energy ~466 kcal/100g, protein ~5.6 g, fat ~24.4 g, carbohydrate ~57.8 g. Beverage component (40% of total weight, 160 g of 400 g) is estimated using USDA FoodData Central entry for hot cocoa prepared with water (~55 kcal/100g, protein ~1.8 g, fat ~1.5 g, carbohydrate ~10.5 g) as a representative mid-range beverage. The drink type at Churros Republic is unspecified; hot chocolate is used as the representative beverage. Composite per-100g values are calculated from the weighted blend. This is an estimation; exact values depend on restaurant formulation.

gi_source.title: Churros GI estimated from analogous fried dough / doughnut category; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for churros was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 74 is estimated from the closest analogous fried dough product (doughnuts, GI ~74–76, glucose reference) as reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of GI and GL Values (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). The beverage component (hot chocolate) has a GI of approximately 40–55; the composite GI for the combo is dominated by the churros component. A blended composite GI of 74 is applied conservatively. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (74 × 155.5 g carbohydrate in full 400 g serving) / 100 ≈ 115. This is a high-GL meal.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~195 mg/100g) and high energy density from fried dough
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size of this fried dough combo, as the sodium content across a full serving (~780 mg for the 400 g combo) represents approximately one-third of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; the high caloric density also contributes to weight-related blood pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~74) and very high glycemic load per serving (~115), driven by refined wheat flour, sugar coating, and deep-frying
type2_diabetes.guidance: This combo is not suitable for regular consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very high glycemic load (~115 per full serving), which will cause a rapid and substantial postprandial blood glucose rise; if consumed, portion should be strictly limited and paired with a low-GI food to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (~15.8 g/100g), saturated fat (~3.5 g/100g), trans fat (~0.1 g/100g), and high energy density from deep-frying in oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this combo as an occasional indulgence only, as the combination of saturated fat, trans fat, and high caloric density from deep-fried dough is associated with adverse lipid profiles and increased cardiovascular risk when consumed frequently.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Churros and Drink Combo for Two is a Spanish-origin dessert-and-beverage set meal sold at Churros Republic in Singapore, consisting of two portions of churros paired with two drinks. Churros are a traditional Spanish fried dough pastry made from a simple dough of wheat flour, water, and salt, piped through a star-shaped nozzle and deep-fried in oil until golden and crisp on the outside with a soft, slightly chewy interior. They are typically finished with a generous coating of cinnamon sugar, giving them a sweet, fragrant, and slightly caramelised exterior. The beverage component is unspecified in the menu listing but is commonly hot chocolate, coffee, or a soft drink at Spanish-style churros outlets. Nutritionally, this combo is energy-dense, high in refined carbohydrates, and high in fat due to the deep-frying process. The glycemic index is high (estimated ~74, analogous to fried dough products), and the glycemic load per full serving is very high (~115), making this a significant postprandial glucose challenge. The combo is not suitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or hypertension. It is best treated as an occasional treat. The dish is popular in Singapore as a dessert or snack option, reflecting the growing presence of Spanish-style dessert cafes in the local food scene. Allergens include gluten and wheat. The combo is generally considered halal-friendly at this outlet.
common_user_queries: What is the calorie count of churros?
What is the GI of churros?
Is the Netflix Combo for 2 at Churros Republic healthy?
How many calories in a churros combo meal?

confidence_reason: Churros nutrient values are based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for churros (NDB 18617), which is an approved primary source for this Western food category. However, the beverage component type is unspecified in the field survey listing, requiring estimation using hot chocolate as a representative mid-range beverage. The composite per-100g values are therefore an adaptation rather than an exact match. GI is estimated from the closest analogous fried dough product (doughnuts) in the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed tables, as no direct churros GI measurement was found. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. The original field survey listing uses a promotional name ('Netflix Combo for 2') which is not a canonical food name; the canonical name 'Churros and Drink Combo for Two' has been assigned. 2. The beverage type is not specified in the source listing; hot chocolate is used as the representative beverage for nutrient estimation based on common pairing at Spanish churros outlets. Exact nutritional values will vary depending on the actual drink served. 3. GI of 74 is an analogous estimate from fried dough / doughnut category per Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct churros GI measurement was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 4. Serving size of 400 g is an estimate: 2 × ~120 g churros portions + 2 × ~80 g beverage liquid equivalent. Actual serving weight at Churros Republic - Funan may differ. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy churros entry. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy churros entry. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy churros entry. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy churros entry. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy churros entry. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy churros entry. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy churros entry. 6. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — churros contain no vitamin C. 7. This is a Spanish cuisine item, not a Singaporean dish; therefore the Singaporean multilingual naming requirements (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) do not apply. Local names are not applicable for this food category. 8. The cuisine_tags from the input (Spanish, Dessert, Beverage, Cake) are noted; 'Cake' is not accurate for churros (fried dough, not cake) and has been excluded from classification. 9. Dietary flag is_vegan set to false as the beverage component (hot chocolate) typically contains dairy; is_dairy_free set to false for the same reason. 10. Price SGD 21.20 is preserved from the source record for reference but is not a nutritional field.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Assigned canonical English food name replacing promotional field survey name.
Iteration 1: Computed composite per-100g nutrient values from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy churros data (NDB 18617) weighted with hot chocolate beverage estimate.
Iteration 1: Assigned GI of 74 from analogous fried dough (doughnut) category per Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed tables; calculated GL per serving.
Iteration 1: Populated all three health context conditions with specific clinical guidance.
Iteration 1: Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes field.
Iteration 1: Confirmed vitamin_c_mg = 0.0 with 'confirmed zero' justification in notes.
Iteration 1: Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency — all aliases_by_language strings present verbatim in flat aliases list.
Iteration 2: Reviewed all hard gates and Q criteria; confirmed PASS on all eight criteria.
